---
title: "Journey Prevention Investigation"
author: "Garrett Jenkins"
date: "2026-06-22"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Loading

``` {r loading_configuration}

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
library(psych)

Daily_Checks <- read.csv("C:/Users/Owner/Downloads/Daily Checkins.csv")

Daily_Checks$DAILY <- 1

colnames(Daily_Checks)[colnames(Daily_Checks) == "CHECKINDATE"] <- "EVENTDATE"

Monthly_Assessments <- read.csv("C:/Users/Owner/Downloads/Monthly Assessments.csv")

Monthly_Assessments$MONTHLY <- 1

colnames(Monthly_Assessments)[colnames(Monthly_Assessments) == "ASSESSMENTDATE"] <- "EVENTDATE"

Platform_Events <- read.csv("C:/Users/Owner/Downloads/Platform Events.csv")

#Comprehensive_DF <- merge(Monthly_Assessments, Platform_Events, by = c('USERID', 'EVENTDATE'), all.x = TRUE)

drop_list <- c("\\[", "\"", " ", "\\]")

pattern <- paste0(drop_list, collapse = "|")

Platform_Events$PLATFORMEVENTS <- gsub(pattern, "", Platform_Events$PLATFORMEVENTS)

Platform_Events <- Platform_Events %>%
  mutate(PLATFORMEVENTS = map_chr(str_split(PLATFORMEVENTS, ","), ~ paste(unique(str_trim(.x)), collapse = ",")))

Platform_Events <- Platform_Events %>%
  separate_longer_delim(PLATFORMEVENTS, delim = ",") %>%
  mutate(PLATFORMEVENTS = trimws(PLATFORMEVENTS), present = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = PLATFORMEVENTS,
    values_from = present,
    values_fill = 0,
    values_fn = max # Ensures a 1 is kept if a word accidentally repeats in a cell
  )

Comp_DF <- merge(Platform_Events, Daily_Checks, by = c('USERID', 'EVENTDATE'), all.x = TRUE)

Comp_DF <- merge(Comp_DF, Monthly_Assessments, by = c('USERID', 'EVENTDATE'), all.x = TRUE)

Comp_DF <- Comp_DF[,-c(35,36,39)]

Comp_DF$EVENTDATE <- as.Date(Comp_DF$EVENTDATE)

###write.csv(Comp_DF, "C:/Users/Owner/Downloads/TEST.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```

## Exploration

### Checking that all Journey Event Types have actually observed events
### They do

```{r include=FALSE}

col_indexes <- c(3:30)

col_sums <- colSums(Comp_DF[, col_indexes])

col_sums

```

``` {r descriptives_daily_stress}

table(Comp_DF$STRESSLEVEL)

```


``` {r coding_indicator_variables}

Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK <- ifelse(Comp_DF$FEELINGRATING == 1, 1, 0)

#Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK <- ifelse(is.na(Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK), 0, Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK)

Comp_DF$STRESS_RISK <- ifelse(Comp_DF$STRESSLEVEL > 5, 1, 0)

#Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK <- ifelse(is.na(Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK), 0, Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK)

Comp_DF$DEP_ANX_RISK <- ifelse(Comp_DF$FEELINGSFREQUENCY > 3, 1, 0)

#Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK <- ifelse(is.na(Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK), 0, Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK)

### Bucket into	moderate, elevated, high-risk
###	Review distribution of daily stress scores > 5 to define

### If stress increases over time by any amount then RISK
### How much increase is moderate, elevated, high-risk?

### If depression/anxiety increases from Never/Almost never to >= “Sometimes” then RISK
### How to define moderate/elevated/high-risk here?



Comp_DF$CHALLENGING_DAYS_RISK <- ifelse(Comp_DF$DAYSEMOTIONALLYCHALLENGING > 0, 1, 0)

#Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK <- ifelse(is.na(Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK), 0, Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK)

Comp_DF$MANAGE_STRESS_RISK <- ifelse(Comp_DF$MANAGESTRESSLEVEL <= 3, 1, 0)

#Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK <- ifelse(is.na(Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK), 0, Comp_DF$BAD_DAY_RISK)

#Comp_DF$RISK <- ifelse(Comp_DF$FEELINGRATING == 1 | Comp_DF$STRESSLEVEL > 5 | Comp_DF$DAYSEMOTIONALLYCHALLENGING > 0 | Comp_DF$MANAGESTRESSLEVEL <= 3, 1, 0)

Comp_DF <- Comp_DF %>%
  group_by(USERID) %>%
  mutate(
    BASELINE_STRESS = first(STRESSLEVEL[EVENTDATE == min(EVENTDATE)]),
    BASELINE_COPING = first(MANAGESTRESSLEVEL[EVENTDATE == min(EVENTDATE)]),
    BASELINE_CHALLENGING = first(DAYSEMOTIONALLYCHALLENGING[EVENTDATE == min(EVENTDATE)]),
    BASELINE_DEP_ANX = first(FEELINGSFREQUENCY[EVENTDATE == min(EVENTDATE)]),
    STRESS_INCREASED = as.integer(STRESSLEVEL > BASELINE_STRESS & EVENTDATE != min(EVENTDATE)),
    COPING_DECREASED = as.integer(MANAGESTRESSLEVEL < BASELINE_COPING & EVENTDATE != min(EVENTDATE)),
    DAYS_CHALLENGING_INCREASED = as.integer(DAYSEMOTIONALLYCHALLENGING > BASELINE_CHALLENGING & EVENTDATE != min(EVENTDATE)),
    DEP_ANX_INCREASED = as.integer(FEELINGSFREQUENCY > BASELINE_DEP_ANX & EVENTDATE != min(EVENTDATE)),
    ANY_RISK = as.integer(any(BAD_DAY_RISK == 1, STRESS_RISK == 1, DEP_ANX_RISK == 1, CHALLENGING_DAYS_RISK == 1, MANAGE_STRESS_RISK == 1, STRESS_INCREASED == 1, COPING_DECREASED == 1, DAYS_CHALLENGING_INCREASED == 1, DEP_ANX_INCREASED == 1, na.rm = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(USERID, EVENTDATE)


### How to define moderate/elevated/high-risk for those who indicate a drop in ability to cope (COPING_DECREASED)


Comp_DF$ANY_RISK <- ifelse(is.na(Comp_DF$ANY_RISK), 0, Comp_DF$ANY_RISK)

second_cols <- names(Comp_DF)[3:26] 

### Event Totals Between Monthly Assessment Types

for (col in second_cols) {
  sum_col <- paste0(col, "_MONTHLY_SUM")
  
  Comp_DF <- Comp_DF %>%
    group_by(USERID) %>%
    arrange(USERID, EVENTDATE) %>%
    mutate(period = cumsum(!is.na(MONTHLY))) %>%
    group_by(USERID, period) %>%
    mutate(!!sum_col := sum(.data[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(-period)
}

### Event Totals Between Daily Check-in Assessment Types

for (col in second_cols) {
  sum_col <- paste0(col, "_DAILY_SUM")
  
  Comp_DF <- Comp_DF %>%
    group_by(USERID) %>%
    arrange(USERID, EVENTDATE) %>%
    mutate(period = cumsum(!is.na(DAILY))) %>%
    group_by(USERID, period) %>%
    mutate(!!sum_col := sum(.data[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(-period)
}

Comp_DF <- Comp_DF %>%
  mutate(across(
      ends_with("_SUM"),
    ~ lag(.x)
  ))

```

## Subsetting to Monthly and Daily Trend Analysis File

``` {r Subsetting_Monthly}

Monthly_DF <- Comp_DF[which(Comp_DF$MONTHLY == 1), -c(3:30)]  ### Update columns here if needed to remove appropriate vars

Monthly_DF_Risk <- Monthly_DF[which(Monthly_DF$ANY_RISK == 1),]

Daily_DF <- Comp_DF[which(Comp_DF$DAILY == 1), -c(3:30)]  ### Update columns here if needed to remove appropriate vars

Daily_DF_Risk <- Daily_DF[which(Daily_DF$ANY_RISK == 1),]

```

``` {r sample_size_frequencies}


### Based on the information Journey has provided, there appear to be approximately 6,700 participants with repeated assessment ### data and roughly 10,600 participants with repeated daily check-ins.


Comp_DF %>%
  count(USERID) %>%
  #filter(n > 2) %>%
  summarise(distinct_users = n())

Daily_Checks %>%
  group_by(USERID) %>%
  summarise(
    sum_daily   = sum(DAILY, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(sum_daily > 1) %>%
  summarise(distinct_users = n())


Monthly_Assessments %>%
  group_by(USERID) %>%
  summarise(
    sum_monthly = sum(MONTHLY, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(sum_monthly > 1) %>%
  summarise(distinct_users = n())


  Comp_DF %>%
  group_by(USERID) %>%
  summarise(
    sum_daily   = sum(DAILY, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum_monthly = sum(MONTHLY, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(sum_daily > 1 & sum_monthly > 1) %>%
  summarise(distinct_users = n())


```

``` {r monthly_out_pred_correlations}

Outcomes <- c("STRESSLEVEL", "MANAGEFEELINGSLEVEL", "FEELINGSFREQUENCY", "DAYSEMOTIONALLYCHALLENGING", "MANAGESTRESSLEVEL")

Pred_Out_M <- Monthly_DF[, which(grepl("MONTHLY_SUM", names(Monthly_DF)) | names(Monthly_DF) %in% Outcomes)]

Cors_list <- NULL

for (i in Outcomes) {

ctest_M <- corr.test(Pred_Out_M)
r_value_M <- round(ctest_M$r[, i], 4)
p_value_M <- round(ctest_M$p[, i], 4)
n_value_M <- ctest_M$n[, i]

Monthly_Cors <- data.frame(Correlation = r_value_M, P_Value = p_value_M, N = n_value_M, Outcome = i)

Monthly_Cors$Predictor <- sub(".*\\.", "", rownames(Monthly_Cors))

  Cors_list[[i]] <- Monthly_Cors
  
}

Monthly_Cors_DF <- do.call(rbind, Cors_list)

rownames(Monthly_Cors_DF) <- NULL

```

``` {r daily_out_pred_correlations}

Pred_Out_D <- Daily_DF[, which(grepl("DAILY_SUM", names(Monthly_DF)) | names(Monthly_DF) %in% Outcomes)]

for (i in Outcomes){

ctest_D <- corr.test(Pred_Out_D)
r_value_D <- round(ctest_D$r[, i], 4)
p_value_D <- round(ctest_D$p[, i], 4)
n_value_D <- ctest_D$n[, i]

Daily_Cors <- data.frame(Correlation = r_value_D, P_Value = p_value_D, N = n_value_D, Outcome = i)

Daily_Cors$Predictor <- sub(".*\\.", "", rownames(Daily_Cors))

  Cors_list[[i]] <- Daily_Cors
  
}

Daily_Cors_DF <- do.call(rbind, Cors_list)

rownames(Daily_Cors_DF) <- NULL

```
